result_summary.name: TTT Power Transition 2026
result_summary.description: Canonical TTT template locked 2026-04-05 after Map+Units finalization. 20 countries, 10x20 global hex grid, 2 tactical theaters (eastern_ereb, mashriq), 345 default units.

final_assessment:
Based on my assessment of Cathay's strategic position and force disposition, here are my **3 TOP MILITARY PRIORITIES** for Q1 2026:

## **PRIORITY 1: Consolidate Anti-Access/Area-Denial (A2/AD) Posture in First Island Chain**

**WHY:** Formosa has concentrated all 6 observable units in hex (7,18) with robust air defenses and ground forces, creating a heavily fortified position. Our carrier-killer missiles at (6,15) are well-positioned, but we need stronger naval presence to credibly threaten any intervention forces. Bharata also shows concerning LAC deployments that could open a second front.

**SPECIFIC ACTIONS:** Activate reserve naval unit cat_n_03 (South Sea Fleet SCS north) to hex (6,18) to create overlapping naval coverage with our existing East Sea Fleet units. Move reserve tactical air cat_a_07 (SCS air) to active status at hex (6,16) to strengthen air superiority in the Taiwan Strait approach lanes.

## **PRIORITY 2: Strengthen Western Border Defense Against Bharata Buildup**

**WHY:** Intelligence shows 4 active Bharata ground units concentrated along our LAC border at hexes (7,14), (7,15), and (8,14), supported by advanced air defenses including S-400 systems. This represents a significant threat to our Tibet/Xinjiang territories and could force us into a costly two-front scenario if tensions escalate.

**SPECIFIC ACTIONS:** Activate reserve ground units cat_g_07, cat_g_08 from WTC (India/Tibet border) and position them at hex (7,15) to counter Bharata's forward deployment. Deploy reserve air defense cat_d_03 (HQ-9 western) to provide integrated air protection for this critical border region.

## **PRIORITY 3: Maintain Nuclear Deterrent Readiness and Deep Strike Capability**

**WHY:** With Level 2 nuclear capability and strategic missile forces, we must ensure credible deterrence against potential coalition formation. Albion naval presence in Mediterranean (7,10) and Arabian Sea suggests possible intervention capability, while Yamato's limited but quality forces could complicate Pacific scenarios.

**SPECIFIC ACTIONS:** Keep strategic missiles cat_m_01 and cat_m_02 at current positions for optimal coverage. Activate reserve strategic missile cat_m_03 (DF-41 mobile interior) to provide mobile deterrent backup and maintain strategic ambiguity about our second-strike capability.

**OVERALL STRATEGIC INTENT:** Deter intervention through credible A2/AD while preventing border conflicts from escalating into multi-front warfare.
